relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: At dawn after Sabbath, the women come to the tomb.
Earthquake and messenger roll back the stone.
Lightning and snow mark heavenly presence.
The guards tremble into deathlike stillness.
The women are told not to fear while seeking the crucified one.
He is not here; he was raised as he said.
They are sent as witnesses to the disciples and toward Galilee.
Fear and great joy run together from the tomb.
Jesus meets them; they grasp his feet and worship.
He calls the disciples brothers and sends them to Galilee.
The guard reports what happened to the priests.
Counsel and silver produce a counter-story.
They are told to say the disciples stole the body by night.
Power promises protection for the false report.
Silver spreads the story.
The eleven go to the appointed mountain in Galilee.
They worship, and some remain divided in trust.
Jesus comes near with all authority in heaven and earth.
The nations are to be discipled and immersed into Father, Son, and holy Spirit.
The risen one remains with them all days until the age’s completion.
